Ad Text:
About the Role

We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced Data Engineer to join a dynamic cross-functional digital team. This role will focus on designing, building, and optimising scalable data systems and pipelines to support complex digital and analytics use cases.

You will play a key role in delivering high-quality, reliable, and secure data solutions across modern cloud-based architectures.

Key Responsibilities
Design, build, and maintain scalable ETL/ELT pipelines to support batch and near real-time data processing.
Develop and optimise data pipelines on GCP, leveraging services such as Cloud Run, Dataflow, Pub/Sub, and Cloud Functions.
Implement event-driven architectures using Pub/Sub and related services to enable real-time data ingestion and processing.
Work with Spark for large-scale data transformation and processing.
Orchestrate workflows using tools such as Airflow / Cloud Composer.
Integrate data from multiple internal and third-party systems, ensuring reliability and scalability.
Ensure high data quality through validation, monitoring, and testing frameworks.
Implement CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure-as-code, and automated deployment practices.
Apply data governance, security, and compliance best practices, including IAM and secrets management.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (engineering, product, analytics) to deliver end-to-end data solutions.
Skills & Experience
3–5+ years of experience in data engineering
Strong proficiency in Python and SQL for data processing and transformation.
Hands-on experience building ETL/ELT pipelines in cloud environments.
Experience with GCP services (Cloud Run, Dataflow, BigQuery, Pub/Sub)
Strong experience with Spark for distributed data processing.
Experience with workflow orchestration tools (e.g. Airflow / Cloud Composer).
Solid understanding of data storage systems 
Familiarity with DevOps and security practices
Experience designing event-driven and microservices-based architectures.
Strong communication skills, with the ability to work with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
This is a Sydney-based hybrid role, and candidate must have full working rights.

Melbourne is Australia's fastest growing city, and public transport is vital to keeping it moving. Since 2009, Metro has proudly operated and maintained a world-class train service and delivered major transport projects that shape the future of our city. Guided by our value of Safely Always, we are one team dedicated to ensuring everyone gets home safely while helping to connect and create a better Melbourne.





ABOUT THE ROLE

We currently have an exciting full time permanent opportunity for a Power Platform Developer to join our OCMS (Technology) team based at 700 Collins street.

In this role you will provide the capability to implement automated software solutions for Metro processes that streamline data collection, document review, and stakeholder approval processes, while also providing the outputs for intelligent, visual reporting through interactive dashboards.

The role will report to the Applications Support Manager within OCMS and will work closely with the Projects Interface Manager within the projects division to provide technical expertise across all applications & workflows.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Establish and participate in prioritisation forums with Projects Interface Manager and Applications Support Manager to successfully plan and implement new and updated solutions
With the support of the Projects Interface Manager and Applications Support Manager, own, maintain and continuously improve all developed software solutions in accordance with Portfolio and Business requirements and agreed priority.
Support the Project Interface Manager and Applications Support Manager to provide exceptional client support to key stakeholders within Portfolio Program teams, Finance and Commercial (F&C), Network Assets (NA) and Engineering and Assurance teams
Perform routine and reactive maintenance activities as well as enhancements and improvement work for applications developed on Microsoft Power Platform, SharePoint / Dataverse, or other development technologies
Support, develop and document ancillary processes associated to these applications, such as Power Automate Workflows, Data Flow Connections, API connections.
Follow OCMS processes through all stages of development lifecycle ensuring appropriate review/approvals are obtained

ABOUT YOU

Relevant Engineering or Science degree or equivalent e.g. Data Science, Process Engineering or Software Engineer
Accredited Microsoft Power Apps Developer PL-400 and functional consultant PL-200 with significant knowledge and experience in developing, maintaining and improving software solutions in both Canvas and Model driven type applications.
Demonstrate experience in design and build of complex data models.
Demonstrate advanced knowledge in the use of Power Automate and the integration into Power Apps.
Demonstrate advanced Microsoft Excel and sharepoint knowledge and ability.
Strong stakeholder engagement skills and the ability to influence positive business outcomes.

WHY METRO

FREE Myki card for PTV travel across Victoria
14 weeks Paid Parental Leave (no qualifying period)
Career progression and training opportunities
Discounts at top brands and stores
Diversity & Inclusion - we value unique perspectives and support initiatives like Women at Metro (over 30% of our workforce are women)

Next Steps

If this role resonates with your values, experience, and career growth, then please apply before the due date and be part of Melbourne's growing rail network!

Applications close Friday, 3rd April 2026 at 23:59pm.

Metro is committed to making reasonable adjustments to provide a positive, barrier-free recruitment process for candidates with specific access needs. Candidates who require adjustments are encouraged to declare this in their application, so that the contact person for the position can discuss your needs.

Ad Text:
A career at ASIC will broaden and deepen your already accomplished knowledge, skills and abilities. By working on cutting edge issues, you'll make a difference to our goals, Australia's economic reputation and wellbeing and your own career aspirations 

 

ASIC's Data Engineering team is seeking a Data Engineer to join their team. The successful candidate will support data delivery and data infrastructure across the organisation.

Sydney or Melbourne locations
Permanent full-time position
Salary from $97,401 to $109,171 (depending on experience) + 15.4 % superannuation
Applications close at 11:59pm AEST, Wednesday 8 April 2026

 

The team 

Information Technology Services delivers a broad suite of IT capabilities and support to ASIC. We are currently driving major technology initiatives that will accelerate our transformation across key areas including virtualisation, data analytics, mobility, and cloud. The Data Engineering team underpins these efforts by providing essential services to our primary stakeholder, the Data & Analytics Office. 

 

The role

As a Data Engineer, you will play a pivotal role in enabling ASIC's data-driven operations. Your responsibilities will include designing and maintaining robust data ingestion pipelines, managing secure and efficient data transfers into ASIC's systems, and ensuring timely and accurate delivery of data to support core business functions. This role is critical to empowering analytics, enhancing operational decision-making, and supporting the broader technology transformation underway at ASIC.

 

More specifically, you will: 

Support enterprise data solutions to ensure ASIC users have access to timely, accurate and trusted information.
Provide ongoing operational support for data infrastructure, including cloud platforms, data lakes, data marts and dashboards.
Monitor data platform performance and recommending infrastructure enhancements to ensure performance benchmarks are consistently met.
Implement ETL pipelines using CI/CD methodologies to enable reliable and scalable data delivery.
Support data projects and initiatives by providing end‑to‑end data engineering services, including impact assessment, design, development, implementation and ongoing support.
Collaborate with system owners and stakeholders to ensure data availability and working closely with business units to gather requirements and deliver effective data solutions.
Communicate data‑related issues, risks, insights and providing regular reporting to leadership. Investigate and resolve data-related incidents promptly.

 

About you

The ideal candidate for this role is a motivated and technically capable Data Engineer who is eager to grow and contribute to ASIC's evolving data landscape. You'll bring hands-on experience with data pipelines, cloud platforms and ETL processes, along with a solid understanding of data infrastructure and performance monitoring. This role is ideal for someone ready to take the next step in their data engineering career while contributing to impactful data and technology transformation projects.

 

In addition, you will have:

Tertiary qualifications in a quantitative discipline such as Statistics, Mathematics, Information Technology, Mechatronics, or a related field.
Familiarity with Business Intelligence solutions (e.g. SSRS, Qlik Sense) and analytics tools and languages (e.g. R, Python).
Demonstrated knowledge of data warehousing and big data architectures, including data streaming and data virtualisation concepts.
Strong development capability in Python and SQL.
Demonstrated communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively with a wide range of internal and external stakeholders and to clearly translate technical concepts for business audiences.
Proven ability to identify, prioritise, and respond to strategic and tactical challenges and opportunities by developing and delivering appropriate solutions.
Experience working within Agile delivery environments.

 

Click ‘apply' to start your application. Applications close at 11:59pm AEST, Wednesday 8 April 2026.

 

About ASIC

ASIC's remit is one of the broadest of regulators across the world. ASIC regulates corporations, markets, financial services and consumer credit and monitors and promotes market integrity and consumer protection in the Australian financial system. Through our enforcement work, we hold to account those who contravene the law, working to achieve strong outcomes that address the greatest consumer and investor harms. 

ASIC is an equal opportunity employer seeking people who want to make a difference. ASIC is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace where the very best talent in Australia chooses to work. Applications from people of all ages, cultural backgrounds, abilities, LGBTIQ+ identity, and people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent are encouraged to apply.

We offer a range of employee benefits including:

Attractive superannuation
Additional leave entitlements
50/50 hybrid work-from home model
Flexible work arrangements
Assistance for study and professional development

 

Click here to view ASIC's salary and benefits guide.

To read more about ASIC, you can visit our website or review our Corporate Plan.

To work with us, you need to be an Australian citizen and be prepared to complete an ASIC Suitability and Baseline Assessment.

📍 East Maitland, NSW (Hybrid)
💰 Competitive salary + benefits + career growth
🚀 Azure | Automation | Enterprise Infrastructure | Transformation

Our Opportunity

We're looking for a Cloud Infrastructure Engineer to join our growing IT team and play a key role in shaping and optimising our enterprise technology environment.

This is a hands-on, senior technical role where you'll design, build and continuously improve our Microsoft cloud and network infrastructure, supporting a national healthcare organisation undergoing exciting transformation.

Working closely with our Head of Cyber Security & Enterprise Infrastructure, you'll help deliver scalable, secure and high-performing solutions across Azure, virtual environments, endpoints, and networking - with a strong focus on automation and modern best practices.

What You'll Be Doing

Cloud & Azure Infrastructure

Manage and optimise Azure services including subscriptions, policies and access controls

Administer Microsoft Entra ID and identity governance in a hybrid environment

Design secure access frameworks in partnership with cyber security


Virtual Desktop & Compute (AVD / VM)

Manage AVD environments including host pools, session hosts and performance

Oversee VM lifecycle including scaling, patching and image management

Deliver reliable, high-performing virtual infrastructure across distributed sites


Endpoint & Device Management

Manage MDM environments across Windows and mobile devices

Improve and automate device provisioning and enrolment

Support endpoint security uplift aligned with Essential Eight


Networking & Connectivity

Support SD-WAN environments and site connectivity across national locations

Contribute to "site-in-a-box" deployments for new and existing centres

Maintain secure, zero-trust aligned network configurations


Automation & Continuous Improvement

Build PowerShell scripts to automate infrastructure and reporting

Contribute to infrastructure-as-code practices (Bicep, ARM templates)

Identify opportunities to streamline operations and reduce manual effort
What You Bring
10+ years' experience in infrastructure engineering, with strong Azure expertise

Proven experience with Microsoft Entra ID, AVD, MDM/RMM and cloud platforms

Strong PowerShell scripting and automation capability

Experience working with enterprise networking or SD-WAN environments

A security-first mindset with exposure to identity-driven and zero-trust frameworks


Nice to have:

Experience with IaC tools (Bicep, ARM)

Azure certifications (AZ-104, AZ-305, AZ-140)

Exposure to PKI / certificate-based authentication
Why Join Us?
Be part of a national organisation making a real impact on health outcomes

Work in a modern, cloud-first environment with ongoing investment in technology

Join a collaborative, high-performing team driving transformation

Hybrid working flexibility

Career growth and development opportunities


Ready to Apply?

If you're a hands-on cloud infrastructure expert who enjoys building, improving and delivering real outcomes - we'd love to hear from you.

Applications close 13th April 2026

Please note: Only candidates with full, permanent working rights in Australia will be considered for this role

Location

This role will be based onsite, out of our Melbourne facility

About the Role

The Maintenance Automation Engineer will play a critical role in the ongoing development, optimisation, and support of Stacked Farm’s advanced automation systems. This position requires hands-on experience with industrial control systems, PLC programming, SCADA platforms (such as Ignition), IIoT technologies, and industrial networking protocols.

Working as part of a fast-paced, innovative engineering team, you will contribute to the design and refinement of automation code, test system functionality, troubleshoot issues, and enhance system reliability for large-scale mission-critical operations. The ideal candidate brings both technical depth and creative problem-solving skills, with a desire to continuously learn and apply modern automation technologies to deliver scalable, efficient, and intelligent automation solutions.

What you'll be doing:

Design, develop, and maintain PLC-based automation code using Structured Text and efficient coding practices.

Develop and implement robust control strategies for process automation using SCADA/HMI systems, particularly Ignition.

Test and validate system functionality to ensure performance, stability, and safety across all operational automation platforms.

Collaborate with electrical, mechanical, and software engineers to ensure seamless integration of automation systems into broader infrastructure.

Troubleshoot and resolve issues in PLCs, HMIs, and industrial networks in real-time environments.

Maintain clear and structured documentation for automation architecture, logic flows, code libraries, and system configurations.

Support version control, testing, and continuous integration workflows across all automation codebases.

Proactively monitor systems, identify improvement opportunities, and deliver enhancements for performance and efficiency.

Stay up-to-date on emerging technologies, standards, and best practices in automation, control, and industrial networking.

Work closely with multidisciplinary teams including software, electrical, mechanical and operations teams.

Collaborate with suppliers and technology partners to implement and refine automation components.

Provide technical insight during project planning and design reviews to ensure automation systems meet operational needs.

Support knowledge sharing and foster a continuous learning environment within the engineering team.

About You

Bachelor’s Degree in Automation Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Mechatronics, Computer Engineering or a related discipline.

3+ years of hands-on experience in industrial automation, PLC programming or control systems engineering.

Demonstrated experience with Ignition SCADA or similar HMI/SCADA systems.

Proficient in structured programming and version control tools (e.g. Git, GitHub).

Exposure to IIoT concepts, architecture, and protocols is advantageous but not essential.

Experience working in an agile, fast-paced, engineering environment.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

Innovative and solutions-oriented mindset with a passion for automation and technology.

Strong communicator who can explain technical concepts clearly to a variety of stakeholders.

Adaptable and eager to learn new tools, technologies, and frameworks.

Takes ownership of outcomes and is committed to excellence in execution.

Works effectively both independently and within a team.

Job Title: Security Design Engineer - Al Defensive Capabilities
Ad Text:
12 Months Contract
Office Location at Docklands VIC
Hybrid Working (2-3 days onsite)
A leading telecommunciations company is seeking for an experienced Security Design Engineer who will be working closely with the Architecture team to design and deliver advanced security engineering capabilities.

You will focus on AI-driven security, offensive-informed defence, and the development of scalable security controls. The role involves translating security requirements and documentation into high-level and low-level designs, while ensuring alignment with enterprise security architecture and regulatory frameworks.

Key Responsibilities:

Work closely with the Architecture team to design and implement security engineering solutions.
Contribute to AI-driven offensive and defensive security engineering initiatives.
Build AI-based attack simulation capabilities that emulate adversary behaviours.
Develop defensive capabilities including behavioural analytics, anomaly detection, and predictive threat modelling.
Convert working manuals and documentation into high-level designs (HLD) and low-level designs (LLD).
Integrate attack simulation outputs into operational defence systems and security tooling.
Support deployment and optimisation of defensive cybersecurity tools and platforms.
Collaborate with penetration testers, red teams, and adversary emulation specialists.
Convert offensive findings into engineered defensive improvements.
Ensure alignment with enterprise security architecture and standards.
Key Requirements:

Prior experience working as a Security Design Engineer, Security Architect, or Solutions Architect.
Strong focus on security architecture and design.
Experience producing high-level designs (HLD) and low-level designs (LLD).
Solid background across multiple cybersecurity domains.
Experience deploying defensive and cybersecurity tooling.
Knowledge of AI/ML concepts applied to security is desirable.
Strong stakeholder engagement and cross-functional collaboration skills.
Experience working in complex enterprise environments.
Telecommunications industry experience preferred.
Ability to obtain and maintain a security clearance.
Contract Rate: $900-$1000 per Day (incl. super)

Lead AI Engineer



Deliver AI-powered solutions that transform the experience for employees and for millions of members
Permanent, full-time position
Flexible, hybrid working environment


At AustralianSuper, we truly care about our colleagues. We know work and life are intertwined. That's why we support the diverse needs of everyone and have policies that enable us all to thrive and be truly flexible. We ensure diversity is celebrated for the opportunity it provides us all to learn and grow and deliver better outcomes for members.



Your New Role


This role is about moving GenAI from proof‑of‑concept to trusted, enterprise‑grade systems used every day - safely, responsibly, and at scale.


As our Lead AI Engineer (internally known as Lead GenAI Systems Engineer), you'll design, build, and deliver production‑ grade AI solutions that move beyond experimentation and into real‑ world impact. You' ll lead the technical design and delivery of GenAI solutions, from LLM and RAG foundations through to agentic AI systems that orchestrate tools, workflows, and decision‑ making in production.


This is a builder‑ leader role. You' ll provide technical leadership for a small, high‑ impact team of AI engineers, while remaining deeply hands‑ on in solution design, development, and delivery. You' ll have end‑ to‑ end ownership from concept and prototyping through to production deployment, adoption, and ongoing improvement.


You'll work closely with product, data, technology, and risk partners to ensure solutions are robust, scalable, secure, and responsibly designed, aligned with enterprise standards and regulatory expectations.


If you enjoy working at the edge of what's possible with AI, this role offers both challenge and impact.



What You'll Need

You don't need to tick every box, but you'll bring strong experience across many of the following areas:



Strong hands‑ on experience building GenAI solutions, including LLM integration, prompt engineering, RAG patterns, and evaluation approaches in production environments.
Demonstrated capability in prompt design and optimisation, including structuring system and user prompts, managing context, reducing hallucinations, and improving output consistency and reliability.
Experience designing and delivering agentic AI systems, such as tool‑ using agents, workflow orchestration, multi‑ step reasoning, and human‑ in‑ the‑ loop patterns.
Hands‑ on expertise with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP), including deploying and operating AI solutions in cloud‑ based environments.
Experience with containerisation and orchestration, including Docker and Kubernetes, to support scalable, reliable AI services.
Advanced programming skills in Python and/or JavaScript, used to build, integrate, and operate GenAI and agentic systems across APIs, services, and workflows.
Demonstrated experience with MLOps / GenAI operations practices, including lifecycle management, deployment pipelines, monitoring, and operational support.
Experience embedding and operationalising Responsible AI and ethical AI principles, applying best‑ practice patterns across GenAI and agentic use cases in enterprise or regulated environments.
Strong software engineering and system design skills, with the ability to balance experimentation with production readiness, performance, cost, and maintainability.
Experience leading technical delivery, providing clear technical direction, reviewing designs and code, and supporting the development of other engineers while remaining hands‑ on.
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills, able to translate complex technical concepts for both technical and non‑ technical audiences.
An innovative, curious, and growth‑ oriented mindset, with a desire to continuously learn and apply emerging AI capabilities in practical ways.
Relevant certifications and experience in financial services or large global organisations are highly regarded.




Life at AustralianSuper

AustralianSuper is committed to colleague development, and we support our people with ongoing learning, coaching and training, as well as career opportunities across our expanding global organisation. We offer generous leave entitlements and promote a blended working environment in which all roles can flex, and we're happy to discuss what this looks like for you.


We cultivate a workplace that champions safety, respect, inclusiveness and diversity. We are committed to supporting our diverse workforce in a way that is inclusive and embraces diversity in all its forms. If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process or the role, please let our recruitment team know.

What's Next

If you're ready to bring your energy, integrity, generosity of spirit and passion for excellent outcomes to a team that loves a challenge, apply now!


Australian or New Zealand citizenship or Australian permanent residency status is required.




Progress powered by purpose.



AustralianSuper may use AI to review resumes and conduct initial phone interviews. This involves processing your resume, cover letter, and voice recording in Australia and the USA to help assess your suitability for the applied role. When invited to an AI phone screening, you may opt for a traditional phone screening instead. For information on how we handle your personal data, please refer to our Candidate Privacy Policy.



https://www.australiansuper.com/careers/candidate-privacy-notice



Agencies please note: this vacancy is being managed directly by AustralianSuper's Talent Acquisition team. We will contact our preferred agency partners should we require additional support. Thank you.

Turn Data Into Decisions That Power Australia's Automotive Industry

Every day, thousands of automotive repairers and parts suppliers across Australia rely on PartsCheck to connect, quote, and transact. We're the market leader in parts marketplace technology, and we're looking for a Data Engineer who wants to build the data foundations behind software that genuinely matters.

This is a role for engineers who care about data done right — reliable pipelines, trustworthy reporting, and infrastructure that's ready for what comes next.

The Opportunity

We're at an exciting inflection point. Our proven PHP platform serves the industry today, and we're building our next-generation architecture on .NET. As our Data Engineer, you'll work across the full data lifecycle — ingestion, transformation, storage, modelling, and consumption — ensuring our data infrastructure evolves alongside the platform.

This isn't a reporting-only role or a data warehouse babysitting gig. It's the best kind of data engineering challenge: building scalable, AI-ready data assets while delivering insights that drive real business and customer outcomes today.

What You'll Be Doing

Designing, building, and maintaining reliable data pipelines that ingest and transform data from diverse internal and external sources

Owning data quality and integrity — establishing standards for completeness, accuracy, consistency, and timeliness

Developing dashboards, reports, and datasets in that deliver clear, actionable insights for internal teams and external customers

Building and maintaining data infrastructure that supports AI and ML use cases — feature stores, training datasets, and inference pipelines

Applying software engineering rigour to data work — version control, testing, modular design, and CI/CD for pipelines

Managing and optimising workloads within Snowflake, including warehouse sizing, clustering, and cost efficiency

Partnering with Product, Engineering, and business stakeholders to translate data requirements into well-structured, governed models

Supporting our platform migration from PHP to .NET, ensuring data integrations evolve with the application

Using AI to accelerate your craft and build amazing outcomes for our customers

What We're Looking For

5+ years of professional experience in data engineering, analytics engineering, or closely related roles

Hands-on experience with Snowflake (or similar) — data modelling, query optimisation, and platform management

Strong experience building reporting solutions, including data modelling, DAX, and Power BI report design

Solid software development background — .NET (C#) experience highly valued; PHP exposure a practical advantage

AWS experience; you're comfortable working in cloud data environments

Strong SQL skills across structured, semi-structured, and unstructured data

Experience with pipeline orchestration tools (Airflow, dbt, or equivalent)

Deep understanding of data modelling patterns — dimensional, Data Vault, or similar

Hands-on experience applying AI and ML techniques to data engineering and analytics problems

Working knowledge of data governance, lineage, and cataloguing practices

Pragmatic mindset — you balance engineering rigour with delivery

Must have current Australian work authorisation

Why PartsCheck?

Market-leading position in a stable, essential industry

Genuine technical challenges without startup volatility

Brisbane-based team that values collaboration over heroics

Executive team with deep SaaS experience who understand engineering

Opportunity to shape data strategy and AI capability during platform evolution

Career growth alongside a company that's investing in its future
